feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How do I read SAP S/4 Hana Security audit logs using ABAP code ?
entry.content:
How do I read SAP S/4 Hana Security audit logs using ABAP code?  What are the tables and functions which can be used to retrieve events data under security audit logs.

I have tried retrieving it from `RSAU_READ_LOG`, but seem like this doesn't exist.


Please help.

My SAP S/4 version is: 1909

entry.title: SAP MDG: Material - Hyperlink for Standard Fields Not Working
entry.content:
HI Folks 🙂

Hope You Guys are doing great 

Occurred with issue in Hyperlink - for Data Model MM

Problem is for standard fields only - Hyperlink (Not navigating to specific field)not working, but Error Messages & Information things are working. 

Looking forward for this solution Or Any Clues to get fix 

 

Thanks,  

Tarun.

entry.title: Accounting document long text
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

As per the note - 3048704, Long texts are stored in ESH_SR_LTXT table, which gets updated by the program ESH_SR_LTXT_REPLICATE. We don't see the Text object for Accounting documents in this program. Is there any way to replicate ESH_SR_LTXT table with Accounting document long texts. Is there any other table where long texts are stored in decoded format.

Thanks,Girish

entry.title: Supplier Invoice S4 HANA Public cloud - CDS view for GST Tax amount & Withholding Tax Amount
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are working on developing a supplier invoice item level report and there was a requirement to add the GST tax amount and Withholding tax amount fields each line item wise.The report has been created using the following standard data sources 

  1. I_JournalEntry
  2. I_SuplrInvoiceItemGLAcctAPI01
  3. I_SupplierInvoiceTaxAPI01
  4. I_ParkedOplAcctgDocPyblsItem
  5. I_Withholdingtaxitem
  6. I_User

We tried fetching the tax amount field from the above cds and was able to get correct tax values for each line item in the supplier invoice item level report when we use the different gst tax codes for different supplier line items. But, we are getting cumulative tax values for each line item if we use same tax code for different line items due this the tax amount showing as overstated.

Is there any standard cds view that could give us the GST tax amount values at line item wise and also the WHT tax amount for supplier invoice at line item wise?

entry.title: Transitioning Custom Catalogs and Groups from Standalone Fiori to Embedded Fiori Deployment
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently operating with a central hub deployment and plan to transition to an embedded deployment model. Could you please provide guidance on how to migrate custom catalogs and groups from our standalone Fiori system to the embedded environment(S4HANA1909)?

Thanks

Nikhil

SAP Fiori SAP Fiori front-end server SAP S/4HANA 

@Jorge_Baltazar @Jocelyn_Dart

entry.title: S/4HANA 2023 Capabilities
entry.content: I wated to ask if there is a way to expose a CBO as an OData v4 service in an S/4HANA On premise, and add an upsert operation to it.

entry.title: Application object BUPA: Table /SCMB/D_BPTMGEN does not exist Message No. R1149
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have Followed the below mentioned steps

1. Ran the Transaction BP

2.Chose the Organization Button on the top to create once.

3.The Error mention in the Subject line appears and it appears for any of 3 button(Person,Organistion and group)

 

Kindly suggest every possible solution for the same


I did as directed in Question User Tags(Application object BUPA: Table SZA1_D0100 does not exist) i.e I ran BUSB and Added added all the fields as I got from SE16N or SE11 for the table   /SCMB/D_BPTMGEN but  still did not work

entry.title: Find IMG linked to Table TCK35
entry.content: Hi! I am looking for the IMG path or table where table TCK35 is pulling data from. I first sought out transaction code OKK4 but it is only linked to Table TCK05 & TCK06, what I need is to find the IMG path of the plant specific valuation variant i.e. table TCK35.

entry.title: Fiori APP "MONITOR MATERIAL COVERAGE net segment"
entry.content: How can exclude the materials with the "stock availability" bar all green ?

entry.title: SAP CAL always same SID
entry.content:
Hi,

I have two HANA systems via SAP CAL one is licensed and the other is still in the test phase, when I want to license the other one, I get the warning that I have already licensed the system with the SID. But SAP CAL always creates the systems with the same SID. What can I do?

Thank you.

entry.title: 2503684 - SAP S/4HANA: new design of the views in CKM3 and the impact on BW/4HANA
entry.content: What is the impact of the new design on BW/4HANA? Does BW/4HANA need to be adapted, and if so, which adaptations are possible?

entry.title: WIP Batch in SAP PP
entry.content:
Hi, does anyone have knowledge about WIP (Work In Progress) batches? If so, please provide a detailed explanation with pictures and attach any relevant documents. I need a scenario for implementations

@Sisn 

Thanks

entry.title: Unable to save nested handling units
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

As part of the goods shipping process, we would like to display the details of the handling units in the packing list as provided in the shipment. Packaging instructions have been created to enable the creation of nested handling units.

To initiate the handling unit creation process, I use the following application :

Once the information is entered, we can see the desired hierarchy based on the packaging instruction. (See image below)

 

After clicking on "Create and save handling unit," we receive confirmation that the handling units have been created and saved. However, after this save, I am unable to retrieve all the information, whether in the inventory or the packing list.

Is there a missing step in the handling unit creation process ?

Could you please assist me with this ?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: SAP Revolution Journey from ECC to S/4 HANA
entry.content:
SAP Revolution Journey from ECC to S/4 HANA

 Introduction: Hey guys, hope you all are doing good. Today, I am here to talk about SAP ERP software. I recently started learning this software with objective to work and build my career in this technology as I came to know that this business ERP is world most popular and highly adoptable by big companies of various industries like- Amazon, Google, big Automobile companies, Steel manufacturing companies, various service company like Banks, IT companies like Accenture, Tech-Mahindra, E&Y, Oil & Gas industries, Production companies etc.

I am a beginner and have a great interest to learn or deep dive into it and thus keep searching for good explanatory blogs on the same by our SAP senior consultant which I got but in a very scatter manner so with thought to provide relevant SAP and its generation revolution information in a very crispy manner which helps many of us like me who wanted to start their career.

Before I start, I heartfully thankful to our mentor Mr. Vikram Fotani who has given me opportunity to learn and grow.

What is SAP- It is called “System Application and Product in Data Processing”. It is an enterprise application software which helps Industries or Companies to run and record their businesses operation end to end on daily basis of any size and types of business operations. It is highly successful ERP software system that includes all the tools and processes which required to run business successfully for any industries or companies which covers Manufacturing Resource, Production Planning, Financial Management & Accounting, CRM, Human Resources, Customer Services, Payments and many more.

It has been a great and successful history, let me give you a small glance of it.

  • It has been founded in year of 1972 in Walldorf, Germany by five IBM’s engineers. It has started with R/2 in 1979 and then came up with R/3 in 1992 which used to call three tier architecture consisting of the database, the application server, and the client later then the most popular product launched in 2004 which was called ERP which was populated with name ECC (ECC stands for SAP ERP Central Component, which is an enterprise resource planning (ERP) system that's part of the SAP Business Suite. It's one of SAP legacy applications. It is an integrated software solution and highly successful product.
  • Further there was a revolution when SAP launched its 4th generation product in 2015 SAP S/4 HANA which is in-memory database platform, it’s an intelligent enterprise resource planning software which can be deployed On-Premises or On Cloud or Hybrid. It has unique feature of real-time data processing and analytics and that makes it very advance revolutionary upgrade to ERP of SAP. Here, S stands for Simple and 4 stands for fourth generation.
  • Year by year SAP have upgraded the S/4 HANA products based on customer feedback and keep on releasing its new updated version which are more uploaded with new feature from its previous version along with fixing of inconsistency in particular product and bugs. Now today we are having SAP S/4 HANA 2023 (108).

SAP S/4 HANA Deployment Options:

SAP S/4 HANA On Premise- In this common model, the customer acquires a license and installs the software in their own data center. It has all industries business solution, extendable, modifiable and yearly innovation get released.

SAP S/4 HANA on Cloud (Public Cloud)- In this model, the customer rents the software for a certain period of time. SAP deploys the software in the SAP-owned data center. Public cloud software typically runs in a multi-tenant server, where multiple tenants, or customers share the resources of the server.

SAP S/4 HANA on Cloud (Private Cloud)- Private cloud software typically runs in a single tenant server, where only a single tenant (customer) uses the resources of the server. Software runs in a private network protected by a firewall, similar to an on-premise system.

The main differences between private cloud and on premise is who has responsibility for maintaining the server, and the license for the software installed on the server.

SAP S/4 HANA Hybrid- In this model, required applications are operated partially by the customer and partially by SAP in the Cloud. Both parts can be linked and integrated with each other.

SAP S/4 HANA Mobile- In this model, mobile devices access On Premise, or Cloud applications.

There is other digital model which can be explained here but that will not much help us being beginner that is why I don’t elaborate them now but in future in my other blog I will cover them as well.

Now, since I have given plenty of information on the ERP software and its history, we all also start thinking how this works in real world for any industry or company. Well, as of now or say till now what I knew is that to implement SAP S/4 HANA ERP for any company SAP has provide a road map and particular methodology to follow step by step and achieve the goal of using this highly useful and adoptable business software. Let’s know about it on high level.

SAP ERP Implementation Methodology-

SAP has provided three methodology and the decision to use or adopt by company as per their need.

What are all those three methodologies-

  1. ASAP Methodology- The Accelerated SAP (ASAP) methodology is a project management framework used to implement SAP solutions. It is designed to help companies streamline the implementation process, reduce risks, and ensure that SAP projects are completed efficiently.

The ASAP methodology consists of five phases:

Phase1-Project Preparation- In this phase we discuss the project, identify resources, identify project objective, Build a qualified and capable project team.

 

Phase2- Business Blueprint- This phase is very important phase as we discuss with business on various business process, Gather the business requirements, prepare the As is and As on.

 

Phase3-Realization- Prepare the system as per the Blue Print, Test the system, Business User do the testing, Training done for Business users.

 

Phase4-Final Preparation- We prepare and ready the system for Go- Live, Legacy data load activity.

 

Phase5-Go-Live and Post Go-live support- In this phase SAP system goes live where business user start doing business transaction live into system and SAP consultant helps in resolving for any issue or error.

 

  1. SAP Activate Methodology- The primary purpose of the SAP Activate Methodology is to provide end-to-end Application Lifecycle Management and to accelerate the initial implementation of SAP S/4HANA. SAP Activate Methodology phases: D-P-E-R-D-R Discover, Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy and Run
  2. Agile Methodology- Agile methodology is very updated one and many organizations are now a days adapting to it. SAP Agile Methodology is a way to manage SAP projects by applying agile principles and best practices to specific organizational needs. This methodology helps to make SAP solutions more efficient, responsive, and flexible.

I am very new to AGILE and have no experience and therefore I prefer not much speak on this topic but in future I will release blog on this topic as well.

 

I have tried to present here in very short journey of learning as much as possible and I will keep releasing in future based on my learning and experience new topics in this space. Thanks to all, please like and subscribe and share feedback to improve. Happy Learning to all.

# SAP S/4 HANA FICO @ SAP Community

entry.title: Archiving question
entry.content: I got a question on archiving. If we are archiving application data for SD - SD_VBAK, SD_VBRK etc....and if the data being archived has attachments(external attachments and BRF+ outputs), even if we select "archive attachments" the actual attachment that is stored in DB/Content Server/Filesystem/External storage like Sharepoint/AzureBlob is not actually getting deleted. I mean data is getting archived and data from output is getting convered to RAW data, data is getting deleted from SAP tables and archived to whichever location we set it up as.  Is this normal in SAP that SAP standard archiving doesn't actually delete the attachments/outputs created thus leaving them as zombies because the link information in DMS is deleted so one cannot know which business object this attachment belongs to. Can you throw some light on how you managed this situation. This way all attachments are left as zombies for all the data that is archived and this seems like a big issue. I don't know how else to analyse this further

entry.title: How to manage odd quantities in Packing BoM
entry.content:
We are manufacturing individual unit as Make to Stock and we release production order from SAP for 100 qty to produce. We have design the system to keep the SFG BoM and FG BoM Seperate.

Now the question is, we are storing 31 Units under 1 pallet and if we divide the 1000 then 32.25 pallets are required. Now 32 pallets will have 31 Units and each unit attached with the serial number as well AND 32nd Unit will only have 8 Units.

How we can manage the situation wherein we can track the pallet-wise qty and serial number inside each pallet with the use of standard SAP.

Our packing will be done prior to it goes to the storage unit.

Can anyone assist us with good SAP Practise to manage the same?

entry.title: Einvoicing for India
entry.content:
Hello,

We are implementing Electronic Customer Invoices for India. Can someone please explain what is the exact difference between the Einvoice for Customers Legacy (SAP Cloud Integration or SAP Integration Suite ) and the new Einvoicing for customers (SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition). We need to understand if there is any difference besides the way to push to Tax authority/Government

Please find the Sap help portal links below;

Einvoice for Customers Legacy

Electronic Invoices (Legacy) | SAP Help Portal

Electronic Customer Invoice

Electronic Customer Invoices | SAP Help Portal

Thanks

entry.title: Maintenance planning overview - Orders for Planning tile
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am using Maintenance planning overview fiori app. In this app, we have section Orders for Planning which gives the list of order that are not yet released. We have different filter option for this tile - By Priority, Activity type, Order type, user status.

I can see the correct report for the first 3 options but for filter option user status I am not getting correct reports as expected. System is keeping the status profile and user status as blank. Is there a setting that needs to be done to update the user status value??. What am I missing??

Thanks,

Shweta Gupta

entry.title: S/4HANA Security Guidance
entry.content:
What are the best practices for implementing the SAP S/4HANA security baseline? We have defined parameters and critical authorization in our SAP ECC systems as per security baseline. Are there any new or crtical parameters or authorizations have been introduced in S/4HANA which we have to take care of and can be mentioned in baseline security guardrails of an organisation.

Happy to hear from anyone who has worked on this and share their experience.

entry.title: Including Total Cash Discount Amount in DMEE File
entry.content:
I'm trying to include the total cash discount amount in the DMEE file but haven't had success with the two fields I've attempted (FPAYH-RSKON & FPAYH-RWSKT).

Could you advise which field should be used to display the total cash discount amount? This functionality is important for the client, and they need to ensure this information is added correctly. Thank you for your assistance.
